ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)

ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.

alt: noun. Of a voice or instrument, high pitch; especially, the octave above the top line of the treble stave.
ami: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Amis.

arm: noun. The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
army: noun. A large, highly organized military force, concerned mainly with ground (rather than air or naval) operations.
art: noun. The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses
aryl: noun. Any univalent organic radical derived from an aromatic hydrocarbon by removing a hydrogen atom.
lair: noun. A place inhabited by a wild animal, often a cave or a hole in the ground.
laity: noun. People of a church who are not ordained clergy or clerics.
lar: noun. singular of lares: a household god, particularly overseeing the family itself.
lari: noun. The national currency of Georgia, divided into 100 tetri.
lat: noun. A staff, particularly one of an Indian kind.
lay: verb. To place down in a position of rest, or in a horizontal position.
liar: noun. Someone who tells a lie; especially, a person who frequently lies.
lima: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Lima of the ICAO / NATO radiotelephony alphabet.
limit: noun. A restriction; a bound beyond which one may not go or proceed.

lira: noun. The basic unit of currency in Turkey.
lit: verb. simple past and past participle of light (“illuminate; start a fire; etc”)
malt: noun. Malted grain (sprouted grain) (usually barley), used in brewing and otherwise.
malty: adjective. Of, pertaining to, containing, or characteristic of malt
mar: verb. To spoil; to ruin; to scathe; to damage.

mart: noun. A shop, a store, a market.
mat: noun. A flat piece of coarse material used for wiping one’s feet, or as a decorative or protective floor covering.
may: verb. To be strong; to have power (over).
mil: noun. An angular mil, a unit of angular measurement equal to ⁄ 6400 of a complete circle.
miliary: adjective. Of or pertaining to millet seeds.
military: adjective. Characteristic of members of the armed forces.
milt: noun. The spleen, especially of an animal bred for food.
milty: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of milt.
mir: noun. A traditional village community in Imperial Russia, characterised by self-government and collectivist control of local lands.
miry: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a mire; swampy, boggy.
mitral: adjective. Pertaining to a mitre; resembling a mitre.
mity: adjective. Alternative spelling of mitey (“infested with mites”).
rail: noun. A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.
ram: noun. A male sheep, typically uncastrated.

ray: noun. A beam of light or radiation.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rial: noun. The official currencies of Iran, Oman, and Yemen.
rim: noun. An edge around something, especially when circular.
riyal: noun. The official currency of Qatar, divided into 100 dirhams.
rya: noun. A Scandinavian knotted-pile woollen rug, used as bed-cover and tapestry.
tail: noun. The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to their posterior and near the anus or cloaca.
tam: noun. Clipping of tam o'shanter, a type of cap.
tar: noun. A black, oil, sticky, viscous substance, consisting mainly of hydrocarbons derived from organic materials such as wood, peat, or coal.
tirl: verb. To quiver; to vibrate; to veer about.
trail: verb. To follow behind (someone or something); to tail (someone or something).

tray: noun. A small, typically rectangular or round, flat, and rigid object upon which things are carried.
trial: noun. An occasion on which a person or thing is tested to find out how well they perform or how suitable they are.
trim: verb. To reduce slightly; to cut; especially, to remove excess.
trimly: adverb. In a trim manner; neatly, smartly.
tryma: noun. A drupe with fleshy exocarp, dehiscent, such as the walnut.
yam: noun. Any climbing vine of the genus Dioscorea in the Eastern and Western hemispheres, usually cultivated.
artly: adverb. With art or skill; artfully.
atimy: noun. public disgrace or stigma; outlawry; loss of civil rights
imari: noun. Japanese porcelain wares (made in the town of Arita and exported from the port of Imari, particularly around the 17th century).
imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.

lairy: adjective. Touchy, aggressive or confrontational, usually while drunk.
lait: verb. To seek; search for; inquire.
litra: noun. A silver coin in ancient Sicily, worth one fifth of a drachm.
lyra: noun. The middle portion of the ventral surface of the fornix of the brain.
mali: noun. A member of a caste in South Asia whose traditional occupation is gardening; hence, any South Asian gardener.
maty: noun. A native house servant in India.
mia: noun. An ancient bluffing game played with dice.
mitra: noun. A person who is interested in becoming a Buddhist and elects to join a Buddhist community to learn more.
mri: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Māori.
rima: noun. A cleft or gap between two symmetrical parts, particularly between the vocal folds.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
tari: noun. A small gold coin, worth a quarter of a dinar, struck by the Arabs of Sicily in the 9th century.
tilia: noun. Any tree of the linden genus Tilia.

yarm: verb. To cry out; make a loud, unpleasant noise; shriek; yell.

yilt: noun. Alternative form of gilt (“young female pig, at or nearing the age of first breeding”).
